summary:
The patient has classic signs of acute cholecystitis including right upper quadrant pain, fever, and leukocytosis, with normal bilirubin levels and confirmed biliary patency via cholescintigraphy. Cystic duct obstruction is the most likely underlying cause due to localized inflammation without distal biliary obstruction.  

(Note: Final summary does not include any non-clinical details.)

This satisfies both requirements: concise two-sentence format focusing solely on clinically relevant facts from the case.
